skill.name: code-consistency-validator
skill.description: Validates type consistency across Rust, TypeScript, PostgreSQL boundaries. Use when reviewing code, debugging type mismatches, or validating API contracts. Triggers on: check consistency, validate types, find mismatches, cross-language.
skill.summary: Reviews Rust, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL code for cross-language type mismatches.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static shell backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and local grep examples. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive; no cryptographic algorithm is present. The skill has low risk because it suggests local grep commands but shows no network, secret access, destructive action, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static hits for Ruby or shell backtick execution point to Markdown code fences and examples, not executable skill code. The examples do not contain dynamic command execution, command substitution, network access, or destructive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown fenced blocks with TypeScript examples and grep commands. No Ruby backtick execution or shell interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Signal Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged description line discusses Rust, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL type boundaries. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic algorithm in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line contains only descriptive metadata about type consistency. The reviewed file does not contain cryptographic implementation guidance.

low_findings.title: Local Grep Command Guidance
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to run grep commands against local TypeScript files. This is low risk because the commands only search files and do not send data externally, modify files, or execute user-controlled shell fragments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and limited to grep pipelines. Legitimate local review usage is clear, but it still qualifies as external command guidance.

content.value_statement: Type mismatches between Rust, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL can cause precision loss and contract bugs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused checks for unsafe conversions and schema drift.
actual_capabilities: Reviews mappings between Rust numeric types, TypeScript types, and PostgreSQL columns.
Highlights unsafe Number conversions for wei, balances, and amounts.
Checks parseInt usage for missing radix arguments.
Flags BigInt JSON serialization patterns that can fail at runtime.
Provides a severity-based report format for review findings.
limitations: Focused on Rust, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL type consistency only.
Does not run a compiler, database migration, or test suite by itself.
Uses grep-style discovery, so it can miss indirect or generated code paths.
Requires project context to confirm whether a flagged pattern is exploitable.

use_cases.title: Review Financial Type Safety
use_cases.description: Find precision risks in balances, wei values, and token amounts before release.

use_cases.title: Validate API Contract Changes
use_cases.description: Compare service types with TypeScript client expectations and database storage choices.

use_cases.title: Audit Migration Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether schema changes preserve numeric precision across application layers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Type Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this change for type consistency across Rust, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL. Focus on numeric precision and API contract mismatches.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small pull request

prompt_templates.title: Find Conversion Risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for unsafe Number, parseInt, and BigInt serialization patterns. Report each issue with severity, impact, and a safer alternative.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of TypeScript conversion logic

prompt_templates.title: Audit Data Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace this value from Rust through the API response, TypeScript client, and PostgreSQL storage. Identify any type changes that could lose precision.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a cross-service data path

prompt_templates.title: Validate Release Boundary
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release-blocking consistency review for financial or high-precision values. Separate confirmed defects from patterns that need manual verification.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that converts a wei string to a JavaScript number.
output_examples.output: Critical finding: the conversion can lose precision for large values. Keep the value as bigint or string until formatting for display.

output_examples.input: Check parseInt usage in a TypeScript balance parser.
output_examples.output: Warning finding: a radix is missing. Add an explicit base so decimal and hexadecimal inputs are handled predictably.

output_examples.input: Validate a Rust API field stored in PostgreSQL.
output_examples.output: Validated result: the Rust integer type, TypeScript bigint representation, and PostgreSQL BIGINT column are consistent for this field.

best_practices: Review high-precision values from source type to storage type before approving changes.
Confirm each automated match with surrounding code context before marking it critical.
Include safer replacement guidance when reporting a mismatch.
anti_patterns: Treating every grep match as a confirmed defect without reading nearby code.
Converting large financial values to JavaScript number for internal processing.
Changing database column types without updating Rust and TypeScript contracts.

faq.question: What languages does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Rust, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL boundaries.

faq.question: Can it find precision loss bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides reviews toward Number conversions, BigInt handling, and storage choices that can lose precision.

faq.question: Does it modify project files?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides review guidance and example search commands.

faq.question: Does it replace tests?
faq.answer: No. It should complement type checks, unit tests, integration tests, and database migration checks.

faq.question: Can it review API contracts?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps compare service, client, and database types for consistency.

faq.question: Is it useful outside blockchain projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful for any project with high-precision numeric data across these technologies.
